# Program: New fabric invented to generate electricity from light and movement

Program: AM

Tue 13 Sep 2016 at 6:25am Tuesday 13 Sep 2016 at 6:25am Tue 13 Sep 2016 at 6:25am

Space to play or pause, M to mute, left and right arrows to seek, up and down arrows for volume. Play

Duration: 2 minutes 40 seconds 2 m 40 s

Presented by

[Bridget Brennan](https://www.abc.net.au/news/bridget-brennan/167002)

A new fabric has been invented that generates electricity from light and movement. Chinese and US scientists behind the prototype technology say that incorporating the fabric into curtains or clothing could easily charge small electronic devices.

## Credits

* [Bridget Brennan, Author](https://www.abc.net.au/news/bridget-brennan/167002)

## Image Details

Household electricity meter measuring power usage ( ABC News )

Program: More from AM

Australia, Science and Technology, Energy Research, Engineering

## Transcript

If you're often stuck out-and-about with low battery and no phone charger, imagine being able to generate power from your t-shirt.

Scientists say they've invented a super-thin fabric that generates electricity from sunlight and movement.

FEATURED:

Professor Zhong Lin Wang, Georgia Institute of Technology in Atlanta
